titleStmt.title: Letter from Barry C. Galvin to Sir Robert Chalmers, 10 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Barry C. Galvin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter form a Cork based solicitor Barry C. Galvin to Under-Secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). After the Easter Rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. The letter is letting Chalmers know that the have also sent an enclosed petition from a Mrs Ellen Regan for permission to trade until the Annual Licensing Sessions.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7933

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Encl. Offices.
36 South Mall, Cork 10th May 1916
Sir,
Licensing Act. We beg to enclose a Petition do His Excellency The
Lord Lieutenant from Mrs. Ellen Regan of Rea, Inniscarra, Co. Cork,
widow, for the permission to trade until the Annual Licensing Sessions. We are,
Sir,
Your obedient servants, Barry C. Galvin & Son. The Under Secretary
Lord Lieutenant
Dublin Castle
Dated 10th day of May 1916
Petition
OF
Mrs. Ellen Regan
TO
His Excellency The
Lord Lieutenant
of Ireland
To His Excellency
The Right Honourable Ivor Churchill
Baron Wimborne
Lord Lieutenant General and General
Governor of Ireland
Dublin Castle.
The Humble Petition
of
Ellen Regan of Rea, Inniscarra,
Co. Cork, Widow
Sheweth: - That your Petitioner is the widow of Daniel
Regan late of Rea, aforesaid, Licensed Publican. That the said Daniel Regan died at
Rea aforesaid on the 18th day of March
1916 leaving his widow (Your Petitioner) and
four children, namely Hannah (aged 12) Timothy
(aged 11) Nora (aged 10) and Jeremiah (aged 8)
him surviving. That the said Daniel Regan died in
poor circumstances, his only means of support
being the profits of the publicans business carried
on by him at Rea aforesaid and those profits
were barely sufficient to provide the necessaries
of life for himself and his family. That your Petitioner being anxious to
continue the business of publican as carried
on by her late husband, but being too poor to
employ a solicitor to act for her in the matter
applied in person at the District Probate Registry
for a Grant of Administration of said deceaseds
personal estate to enable her obtain a transfer of
the license attached to said premises into her
own name and on the 5th day of April 1916.
she applied at Coachford Petty Sessions Court for
and obtained an ad interim transfer of said
license. That your Petitioner at the time of her
application for said ad interim transfer was
erroneously under the impression that the
next Licensing Sessions for the County of Cork,
East Riding, at which she would apply to the
County County Judge for the transfer of said
license, would be held some time in June. That your Petitioner has now ascertained
that the next Licensing Sessions will be held on
the 23rd day of May instant and as the
Licensing notices in connection with her
application should have been served twenty
one clear days before the said 23rd day of
May she is debarred from applying for the
transfer until the following Licensing Sessions
which wont be held until the 18th day of
October next. That under the circumstances as stated
your petitioner will be obliged to cease carrying
on the trade of publican in the premises
until next October unless Your Excellency
will graciously order that she may be at
liberty to continue to carry on said trade in
the meantime and thus enable her to maintain
herself and her said four children. That if she will be obliged to close
the premises until next October she will be
deprived of her only means of livelihood for
herself and said children. Both she and her
said children would then be in destitute
circumstances and would have no alternative
but to seek poor law relief on enter the workhouse. Your Petitioner therefore
humbly prays that your Excellency
may order that she be at
liberty to continue to carry on
the trade of licensed publican
in said premises pending her
application for a transfer of said
license at the Licensing Sessions
for Cork County E.R. to be
holden on 18th October next.
Dated this 10th day of May 1916 Ellen Regan
